name: Greek Dolmades
recipeInstructions:
1. Unfold grape leaves and rinse with cold water. Remove and discard the small stem from each leaf. Pat dry and set aside.
2. Bring rice and broth to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until the rice is tender, about 25 minutes. Let cool to room temperature.
3. Saute onions in 3 tbsp olive oil until translucent, about 10 minutes, stirring frequently. Stir in garlic, dill, parsley, and half the lemon juice, then remove from heat.
4. Add onion mixture to rice. Season and mix well.
5. Preheat oven to 350°F.
6. On a flat surface, place one leaf smooth side down with stem towards you. With wet hands, roll one tablespoon of rice mixture into a cylinder shape.
7. Place the filling at the stem end of the leaf and begin rolling the leaf, folding both sides. Continue rolling to enclose the filling and make a tight bundle.
8. Place filled leaf in a 9-inch X 13-inch baking dish. Repeat with remaining grape leaves and rice mixture.
9. Drizzle with remaining oil and juice. Cover with foil and bake until tender, about 30 minutes.
10. Remove from oven, allow to cool for 10 minutes, and refrigerate. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
